other_observers: Walter Szeliga, John Vanderpoel, Hannah Woolf, Tom Wilberding, Bill Schmoker, Doug Faulkner; Ted Floyd
specific_location: Extreme SW corner of Boulder Reservoir
description: Bright, luminescent orange legs; sturdy dark bill, fairly thick at the base and then slightly down-curved; overall very brown in appearance, especially compared to a very gray Lesser Yellowlegs standing nearby; about the same size as the Lesser Yellowlegs, but very, very stocky in comparison with a humpy back that made the head seem fairly small; fairly plain head; scapulars were all black with a uniform light brown edge; breast was heavily marked with billowing brown; white vents with dark smudges starting at about the legs; wings reached just barely past the tip of the tail; did I say bright orange legs?Behaviors: Feeding along muddy reservoir shore line in the snow.Call: n.a.Plumage: alternate
